doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_set4 (mpfr/src/set.c L25-L64): the load-bearing set primitive that takes an explicit signb override. mpfr_set delegates here with signb = SIGN(b); mpfr_abs with +1; mpfr_neg with -SIGN(b); mpfr_setsign with the caller's chosen sign; mpfr_copysign with SIGN(c). The function copies b to a at a's precision, but with the caller-supplied output sign rather than b's sign, and uses signb (the OUTPUT sign, not b's) as the rounding direction. The output exponent and the singular-case kind come from b. Algorithm: (1) NaN: ternary=0 with NaN sentinel (the canonical NAN_VALUE; the TS schema drops the C-side sign). (2) Inf/Zero: kind preserved, sign = signb, ternary=0. (3) Normal with prec == b.prec: lossless pad, ternary=0. (4) Normal with prec != b.prec: round via the substrate roundMantissa, passing signb (not b.sign) as the rounding-direction sign. The TS port mirrors the C algorithm at the I/O contract level: the substrate roundMantissa handles the bit-level round-bit/sticky-bit work; the public function dispatches on kind and forwards the explicit signb argument.
c_signature: int mpfr_set4(mpfr_ptr a, mpfr_srcptr b, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode, int signb)
divergence_from_c: C takes (mpfr_ptr a, mpfr_srcptr b, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode, int signb) — `a` is the output slot, mutated in place; precision comes from `a`. TS takes (b, prec, rnd, signb): prec is an explicit positional bigint, returns Result {value, ternary} instead of mutating `a` and returning int. The C side asserts MPFR_ASSERT_SIGN(signb) (signb in {-1,+1}); TS uses the Sign discriminated type (1 | -1) which is structurally narrowed at the public boundary (validateArgs throws MPFRError('EPREC') for anything else). C's NaN return sets ERANGE; TS surfaces NaN via the schema-canonical NAN_VALUE (sign=1, prec=0n per src/core.ts; signb is discarded on NaN — the C-side NaN sign bit is not represented in the schema). For Inf/Zero with non-default signb: C mutates a's sign bit; TS returns a fresh posInf/negInf or posZero/negZero per the signb argument. For normal with prec != b.prec, the C code uses MPFR_RNDRAW with signb as the rounding-direction sign; the TS port calls roundMantissa(..., signb, rnd) which uses signb (not b.sign) to determine whether RNDU/RNDD round away or toward zero. C's overflow branch (++MPFR_EXP(a) > __gmpfr_emax) is mirrored by the substrate's carry-out branch which bumps exp; the public surface delegates to mpfr_overflow only if the substrate's exp exceeds EMAX_DEFAULT. No global flag state (TS is pure).
refs: mpfr/src/set.c L25-L64 — the C reference body for mpfr_set4.
mpfr/src/set.c L66-L79 — mpfr_set delegates here with SIGN(b).
mpfr/src/set.c L81-L96 — mpfr_abs delegates here with SIGN_POS.
mpfr/src/setsign.c L25-L38 — mpfr_setsign delegates here with the caller's bit.
mpfr/src/copysign.c — mpfr_copysign delegates here with SIGN(c).
src/internal/mpfr/round_raw.ts — substrate primitive; takes sign explicitly for RNDU/RNDD direction.
src/ops/neg.ts — sibling op (signb = -SIGN(b)); documents the rounding-sign distinction at length.
src/ops/setsign.ts — sibling op (signb chosen by caller).
src/core.ts L113-L135 — locked MPFR shape with Sign 1|-1.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: Signed zero is real' — sign of zero must propagate per signb.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: Ternary flag is the sign of (rounded - exact)' — at the OUTPUT sign (= signb), not b's sign.
